name: bhccn_10_2032_callable_bloomberg
description: Bloomberg DES/YAS/OAS/ASW BHCCN USD 10% 2032 callable 1st-lien corporate bond captured from screens on 2026-05-03.

provenance.source_detail: Bloomberg DES/YAS/OAS/ASW for BHCCN 10 04/15/32 Corp, USD 144A 1st-lien callable corporate bond, settlement 2026-05-05, price 103.3975, curve 490 SOFR OIS for ASW, OAS curve S23, Treasury benchmark T 3 7/8 04/15/29.
provenance.regen_command: manual Bloomberg DES/YAS/OAS/ASW screen transcription; see provenance.screenshots

screenshots.description: Bond description with identifiers, coupon, maturity, ratings, issuance data, and first coupon metadata
Call schedule showing 2028, 2029, and 2030 callable dates and prices
Coupon information showing long first coupon and normal last coupon
Yield and spread analysis with price, yield-to-worst, spreads, duration, convexity, DV01, accrued, and invoice
Option-adjusted spread analysis with OAS, volatility, option value, vega, call schedule, and OAS risk table
Asset swap pricing tab with bond terms, swap terms, z-spread, yield, ASW spread, MMS spread, and swapped spread detail
Asset swap cashflow table with leg cashflows, discount factors, PVs, accrued interest, and settlement cash
Cash flow analysis tab showing workout cashflows to 2030 call date
Present values tab with spot rates, per-cashflow PVs, and curve YCGT0025
USD SOFR swap curve builder short-end cash, serial futures, and swap quotes for curve 490
USD SOFR swap curve builder long-end swap quotes for curve 490
USD SOFR stripped curve front and intermediate zero rates and discount factors for curve 490
USD SOFR stripped curve long-end zero rates and discount factors for curve 490

meta.schedule_anchor_note: The executable schedule is anchored on 2025-04-15 to match Bloomberg regular coupon dates and 20-day accrued-interest display after the long first coupon has passed.

source_reference.risk_convention_note: Bloomberg screen values are retained directly as expected_outputs. Finstack may use signed risk conventions for some metrics; any mismatch is intentional evidence for Bloomberg parity work.

npv.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg invoice total is displayed to cents and is retained as the assertion target.

clean_price.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg clean price is 103.3975 on 1MM face, displayed to 0.0001 price points.

dirty_price.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg invoice total is displayed to cents and equals clean price plus accrued interest.

accrued.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg accrued interest is displayed to cents at settlement 2026-05-05.

ytm.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg OAS screen displays to-maturity yield to three decimals in percent.

ytw.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg workout is the 2030 call at 100.00 and displays the same yield-to-worst.

z_spread.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg YAS Z-spread is workout-based for this callable bond; the source-backed USD SOFR stripped curve brings the model within 1bp.

i_spread.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg YAS I-spread is workout-based for this callable bond; tolerance allows the remaining small difference between visible stripped-curve zero interpolation and Bloomberg internal swap interpolation.

oas.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg OAS screen is source-backed; tolerance is capped at 2bp while matching the semiannual OAS quote convention.

asw_market.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg ASW screen is source-backed; tolerance is capped at 2bp while matching the workout asset-swap convention.

duration_mod.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg modified duration is displayed to three decimals; tolerance allows for the YTW solver's more precise workout yield.

convexity.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg convexity is displayed to three decimals.

dv01.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg DV01 is displayed as 333 on 1MM face.

yield_dv01.tolerance_reason: Same Bloomberg DV01 source value asserted under the explicit yield-basis metric.

embedded_option_value.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg option value is source-backed; residual gap (~14%) is inherent to BDT model differences (step alignment, calibration details) vs Bloomberg's proprietary lognormal tree while OAS matches within 1bp.

vega.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg OAS screen displays vega to 0.01; residual gap reflects the same BDT model differences that drive the embedded option value gap.
